free_text: in German
-Investigations of the energy dependence of neutron
induced reactions in the energy range of 12-19 MeV.-
(In German).
Facility description.
Rev.1, part A.
Data of this Entry were presented in Figures of this
report UCRL-50400.
3 MeV Van de Graaff.
150 kV Cockcroft Walton Accelerator.
Deuteron-tritium reaction. Thin tritium
containing titanium layer. This layer was regularly
replaced by a new one in order to avoid d-d-reactions
by accumulation of deuterium in the target.
Results compared with statistical theory.
Data taken from NEUDADA.
G.C.
M.M. BIB information was added.
Free text was corrected to be human-readable.
Subents 010-012 were added from Entry 21344, Subents
002-004 (merging in according to NDS IAEA request).
SD: MONITOR units were changed in Sub.2.
Background.
Activation analysis. Measurements at different
incoming neutron energies are done simultaneously in
order to avoid renormalizations to the same deuteron
flux, which increases the error.
Long counter for neutron flux determination.
Boric acid counter for the detection of the  gammas.
From text of article:
at EN=19.6 MeV the c-s is 62mb +- 12%.
Total error contains statistical error,
normalization error (i.e. statistical error of the
reference measurement + uncertainty of the reference
cross section), uncertainties due to Compton tailing
under the photopeak of higher gamma energies and
uncertainties about the level schemes. In some cases
the superposition of gamma lines of different isotopes
with nearly equal halflives adds to the uncertainty.
Data taken from NEUDADA.
Data are presented in Fig.1 of ZP,174,1.
Data are presented in Fig. of REL-REF UCRL-50400,

Ca-W-O(4)
Calculated from measured cross section of CaWO4
using ASSUM-MAX.
Absolute measurement. Neutron flux measured using
a Li-6-I(Eu) scintillation crystal.
Protons not detected since their stopping power
is too low.
Same as sample, Ca-W-O(4) layers of
35 to 65 mg/cm*cm on a non Tl-activated Cs-I-crystal
(20 mm diam * 2 mm thick) as light conductor.
Data taken from NEUDADA.
